_meta.note: Warm conversational tone · curly quotes only · glossary locked

meta_001: The 36 questions from psychologist Arthur Aron’s 1997 experiment — three sets that go deeper and deeper, ending in 4 minutes.
html_003: One phone between you. Take turns asking —
html_007: Set 1 → 2 → 3 — thirty-six questions that go deeper and deeper.
html_008: And at the very end, 4 quiet minutes of looking into each other’s eyes.
html_015: (Personality and Social Psychology Bulletin, vol. 23, no. 4), the exact wording they used. A 2015 New York Times column
html_016: made them known around the world. With the card’s
html_017: button, you can see the original English wording too.
html_018: Don’t let just one of you answer — for each question,
html_024: Tap the screen and the next envelope arrives
html_025: Tap here! Open the first question ✉️
html_028: 🧺 Picnic for Two — go see the other games →
js_001: If you could invite anyone in the world, who would you want as a dinner guest?
js_002: Would you like to be famous? In what way?
js_003: Before you make a phone call, do you ever rehearse what you’ll say? Why?
js_004: What would a “perfect” day look like for you?
js_005: When did you last sing to yourself? And to someone else?
js_006: If you could live to 90 and keep either the mind or the body of a 30-year-old for the last 60 years, which would you choose?
js_007: Do you have a hunch about how you’ll die?
js_008: Find three things you and I seem to have in common.
js_009: What in your life are you most grateful for?
js_010: If you could change one thing about how you were raised, what would it be?
js_011: Take a few minutes and tell your life story — just the parts that matter.
js_012: If you could wake up tomorrow with one new ability, what would it be?
js_013: If a magic crystal ball could tell you anything, what one thing would you ask?
js_014: Is there something you’ve long dreamed of doing? Why haven’t you done it yet?
js_015: What’s the greatest accomplishment of your life?
js_016: What do you think matters most for a true friendship?
js_017: Tell me about your most treasured memory.
js_019: If you found out today you had one year to live, what would you change about how you live? Why?
js_020: What does true friendship mean to you?
js_021: How big a part do love and affection play in your life?
js_022: Take turns naming one thing you like about each other — up to five each.
js_023: Was your family close? Was your childhood happier — or harder — than most?
js_024: How do you feel about your relationship with your mother?
js_025: Each say three honest sentences that start with “We both …”.
js_026: Finish this sentence: “I wish I had someone I could share my ______ with.”
js_027: If we were to become close, what’s one thing I should know about you?
js_028: Tell each other what you like about one another — more honestly than you ever could with a stranger.
js_029: Share the most embarrassing moment of your life.
js_030: When did you last cry in front of someone? And on your own?
js_031: Tell me one thing you already like about me.
js_032: What’s a topic too serious to ever be joked about?
js_033: If you died tonight with no chance to reach anyone, whose unsaid words would you regret most? Why haven’t you said them?
js_034: Your house is on fire. Everyone and every pet is safe — if you could grab one object, what would it be?
js_035: Whose death in your family would hit you hardest? Why?
js_036: Share a personal problem and ask for advice. Then ask the other person to reflect back how you seem to feel about it.
js_037: If you could teleport right now, where would you go?
js_038: If you could take just three things to a desert island?
js_039: Best movie or show you’ve watched lately?
js_040: If you were reborn as something other than a person, what would you be?
js_041: If you had to eat one food for the rest of your life?
js_042: First thing you’d do if you won the lottery jackpot?
js_043: What did you want to be as a kid? Far from where you are now?
js_044: One app you could never delete from your phone?
js_045: When did you last laugh really hard?
js_046: If you could time-travel — past or future?
js_048: If $1,000 landed in your account right now, what would you spend it on?
js_049: If you could have one superpower?
js_051: Your go-to karaoke song, every single time?
js_053: Do you pack days ahead, or right before you leave?
js_054: What’s your profile pic right now? Why that one?
js_056: When did you last cry? (crying at a show counts)
js_058: What’s your MBTI? Does it fit you?
js_059: The country you most want to visit?
js_060: If you were invisible for a day, what would you do?
js_062: Which animal is most like you?
js_063: How do you blow off stress?
js_066: If you could only listen to one artist forever?
js_067: Any secret trick when you make instant noodles?
js_068: The most ridiculous lie you’ve ever been told?
js_069: The single best decision you’ve ever made?
js_072: If you got famous, what would you want to be famous for?
js_073: If your house caught fire, first thing you’d grab? (people and pets aside)
js_074: Who’s your oldest friend? How did you two meet?
js_075: What are you most into these days?
js_076: If you got a whole month of unlimited vacation, what would you do?
js_077: Good sense of direction? Worst time you ever got lost?
js_078: Any board game or game you love?
js_079: The most recent dream you can remember?
js_080: If you could live one day as someone else, who?
js_081: Can you cook? What’s your signature dish?
js_082: What kind of coffee or drink do you like?
js_083: Do you have a favorite word?
js_084: What do you notice first when you meet someone?
js_085: The best place you’ve ever traveled to?
js_086: What scared you most as a kid?
js_087: If you could call anyone right now, who would it be?
js_089: If you were a color, which one would you be?
js_090: What do you most feel like doing on a rainy day?
js_091: If you could master one foreign language?
js_093: The bravest moment of your life?
js_094: If you opened a restaurant, what would you serve?
js_095: The one possession you treasure most?
js_096: What song instantly lifts your mood?
js_097: A hobby that makes time just vanish for you?
js_098: The last time you thanked someone — what was it for?
js_099: What kind of driver are you?
js_101: Something you’ve grown to love as an adult?
js_102: Your favorite cartoon or game as a kid?
js_103: One book that stuck with you for life?
js_105: Something you really want but it’s too pricey to buy?
js_106: Do you prefer time alone or time together?
js_107: What do you think is your biggest strength?
js_108: If you got a pet, what would you want?
js_109: The best compliment you’ve ever gotten?
js_111: A city you’d like to live in someday?
js_112: A childhood dream job that still sounds cool now?
js_113: The most recent new thing you tried?
js_114: A hidden food spot only you know about?
js_115: What’s your role in your friend group?
js_117: One thing you want most right now?
js_118: What draws you to someone? (a small thing, not personality)
js_120: Do you like working out? What kind?
js_121: Give me one of your funniest cringe moments!
js_122: A seasonal smell you love? (like autumn leaves)
js_123: If you wrote a book, what would it be about?
js_124: The most comforting words someone gave you recently?
js_125: Something you always listen to on your commute?
js_126: Do you have a life motto?
js_128: A little secret you haven’t told even close friends?
js_129: The most stunning view you’ve ever seen — where?
js_130: Five years from now, where do you see yourself and what are you doing?
js_131: How do you tend to show someone you care?
js_132: The moment you were proudest of yourself?
js_133: Right now, in this moment, who are you most grateful for?
js_134: What kind of grown-up do you want to be? (or already are?)
js_135: The best moment of your day today?
js_136: What are you most looking forward to on this trip?
js_144: — you were here. Come back to this number next time
js_152: Well done. Remember exactly how this feels right now.
jst_001: whether the envelope is open and the question is showing
jst_005: get the first question ready behind the envelope
jst_008: the flap swings wide open and the seal disappears
jst_009: the envelope vanishes and the letter
jst_011: a new envelope slides up from below
jst_013: already-seen question, so reopen it right away
jst_016: minutes of silently looking into each other’s eyes.
jst_017: A lot of people cried at this moment. Ready? Let’s begin!
jst_018: Press the button and the 4 minutes begin
jst_023: If the conversation never broke off, that’s a win.
jst_024: Shuffle once more for a fresh order?

_app.title: 36 Questions to Open Up
_app.desc: The psychology-backed conversation — 36 questions that deepen, ending in 4 minutes of eye contact.
